title: caucalis royenii
intro: Caucalis royenii, also known as Wild Carrot or Queen's Cup, is a perennial plant that belongs to the carrot family (Apiaceae) and is native to Europe and Asia. The plant has been used for centuries in traditional medicine, particularly for its antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to alleviate conditions such as menstrual cramps, arthritis, and respiratory issues. Caucalis royenii has been shown to have several health benefits, including reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. Herbal preparations of Caucalis royenii can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are often used to treat a variety of ailments, including fever, coughs, and skin conditions.
